statement.markdown: Given an integer array nums of size n, write a function to return true if the array contains a duplicate of any triple element. A triple element is an element that comes three times in a row in the array.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be given as an array of integers, where n is the length of the array.
statement.outputFormat: Return true if the array contains a duplicate of any triple element, false otherwise.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input array is not null and the size of the array is at least 3.

examples.explanation: The triple element 1 and 2 appear twice each in a row, so the function should return true.

hints.text: Use a hash map to count the frequency of each element.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Duplicate Elements
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved by iterating over the array and counting the frequency of each element using a hash map.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach can be used by checking every element with its next two elements.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a hash map to count the frequency of each element, and check if any element appears three times in a row.
